original_image_tags: Hand-drawn diagram illustrating a railway gauge switching operation at the Czechoslovakia-USSR border. The diagram shows a track running horizontally across the page.
Stamped classification mark at bottom right of diagram area.

elements.description: A hand-drawn diagram titled 'DIAGRAM OF RR GUAGE SWITCHING OPERATION AT COP 12 OCT 1955'. It illustrates a railway gauge switching facility with a central rectangular building containing pit under tracks and floor trap doors. It shows two parallel railway tracks: 'CZECH STANDARD GUAGE TRACK' on the left and 'RUSSIAN BROAD GUAGE TRACK' on the right. Components include two 'CZECH RR TRUCKS' and two 'RUSSIAN GUAGE TRUCKS'. A single 'RR SLEEPER CAR MARKED ON SIDE "MOSCOW - PRAGUE"' is depicted. On the right track, a 'RED STAR OVER TRACK' is labeled '(PROBABLE OFFICIAL BORDER MARKER)'. Arrows indicate the direction towards 'CZECHOSLOVAKIA' (left) and 'USSR + COP' (right). Text annotations describe the process: 'CZECH RR TRUCKS ARE PUSHED TO RUSSIAN TRUCKS, BANGING THEM IN DIRECTION OF USSR' and 'FLOOR TRAP DOOR IN CONDUCTOR'S COMPARTMENT WHERE TRUCK DISCONNECT BAR IS LOCATED'.
